---
title: "Day 4: mNGS in Clinical Microbiology"
author: "Minh-Quan Ton-Ngoc, Hao Chung The"
date: "2026-05-15"
---

Day 4 introduces Next-generation Metagenomic sequencing (mNGS) as a practical tool for surveillance, diagnosis, and clinical decision-making in clinical microbiology. The lessons use compact teaching re-analyses so that you can focus on the workflow and analysis results interpretation.

In [mNGS in clinical microbiology](vietnamese-healthy-gut-microbiome.qmd), you will work with a six-sample demonstration re-analysis adapted from Pereira-Dias et al. The exercise uses a subset of a Vietnamese healthy gut microbiome and antimicrobial resistance surveillance dataset to show how shotgun metagenomic reads can be processed into taxonomic and AMR summaries. This is a teaching demonstration, not a full reproduction of the original paper.

In [mNGS in pathogen detection](clinical-reasoning.qmd), you will move from surveillance to a clinical metagenomics case. The lesson focuses on when unbiased sequencing is useful, how infection results should be interpreted, and how metagenomic evidence supports clinical reasoning rather than replacing it.

::: {.callout-note}
## Technical note

The Day 4 practical exercises use R 4.5.0 and Python 3.13. The Python examples also require `numpy` and `pandas`.
:::

## Lessons

**mNGS in clinical microbiology:**

1. [Workflow Setup](vietnamese-healthy-gut-microbiome.qmd)
2. [Taxonomy Analysis](vietnamese-healthy-gut-taxonomy.qmd)
3. [AMR Analysis & Summary](vietnamese-healthy-gut-amr.qmd)

**mNGS in pathogen detection:**

1. [Clinical Case](clinical-reasoning.qmd)
2. [Signal Analysis](clinical-reasoning-analysis.qmd)
